insight: Eviscerator is considered potentially overtuned by masn6811, who calculates it provides approximately 1.23x value relative to its cost (assuming R=1.4 and XV=2.7), approaching Shadowfang-level efficiency (~23% above cost) but without Shadowfang's punishability — if the opponent counters with 5 attack vs Eviscerator, the owner loses the unit but can keep playing, whereas committing 5 attack to stop a Tatsu rush ends the game.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about Eviscerator balance during playtest event with new units, comparing its efficiency to Shadowfang and Hannibal Bull.

insight: Eviscerator self-counters: buying one early is risky because the opponent's Eviscerator can reach 5 attack and kill it, giving the opponent an advantage from the lifespan differential. Optimal play involves not buying Eviscerators early when mirrored, similar to Arka dynamics.
author: consensus
context: silentslayers and elyot discussing Eviscerator balance in Base+1 context, noting the mirror dynamic.

insight: Eviscerator is countered by Felid; Vore counters Automaid; Eviscerator counters Vore; and Ebb is synergistic with all four of these units (enabling blue mana after Automaid harass, improving Eviscerator rushes, and general energy synergies).
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 summarizing the rock-paper-scissors interaction web between new experimental units.

insight: The 1B version of Automaid (tested during the event) was deemed degenerate by masn6811 and xyotsuba: it is only useful in degenerate all-in rushes or occasionally as early harass, wastes a set slot in normal games (poor soak value, only good when blue is free), and forces T1 Blastforge responses, making it unhealthy. The prior 2B Vigilant version was considered well-balanced by masn6811.
author: consensus
context: Extended playtesting of the broken 1B Automaid event variant, with masn6811, xyotsuba, silentslayers, and elyot discussing its degenerate properties.

insight: Automaid (1B version) punishes DDE (Double Drone Engineer) openings in a manner similar to red rush units, since its cheap blue cost means the opponent must respond with a fast Blastforge, making third Engineer effectively unplayable in some sets.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 analyzing why the 1B Automaid warps opening theory, referencing a replay where BB threat invalidated DDE.

insight: Felid is considered slightly overtuned at its current cost; masn6811's math indicates it is undercosted by roughly half a gold. The option of 7RR cost was discussed as a possible nerf direction, though considered too weak by masn6811. A 3-attack change was flagged as another candidate. Velizar notes it is strong enough that you should always buy it, and it becomes even stronger once above the absorb barrier.
author: consensus
context: Multiple players discussing Felid balance during the December 2020 event, with masn6811 doing cost math and velizar/silentslayers sharing gameplay impressions.

insight: Eviscerator is analogous to Shadowfang in offensive efficiency but without Shadowfang's punishability: countering Shadowfang with 5 attack ends the game for the attacker, whereas countering Eviscerator with 5 attack merely removes the unit while the opponent continues. This asymmetry makes Eviscerator stronger in practice relative to its nominal efficiency.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 and elyot debating why Eviscerator may be overtuned despite being designed as an attack-counterable unit.

insight: The Lancetooth at 6BXX cost is described as 'revoltingly strong' by silentslayers and masn6811, while the 8BX version feels quite strong but fair in event games. The analogy drawn is that Lancetooth at its stronger cost is Bloodrager-level efficient but not Bloodrager-level punishable, mirroring the same design concern raised about Eviscerator and Shadowfang.
author: consensus
context: masn6811 and silentslayers comparing Lancetooth variants during the December 2020 event, noting the efficiency-punishability imbalance.

insight: In sets with Vore and Perforator (base set + perf + vore), P2 T1 Animus beats P1 T2 Animus; whereas base set + Perforator alone is likely a P1 win. Base set + Vore alone is a P2 win. The presence of Vore specifically enables the P2 T1 Animus line to succeed.
author: consensus
context: masn6811, silentslayers, and elyot discussing fastimus opening theory in the context of the new event units.

insight: Cryo Ray at Lifespan 5 and 2 HP was proposed as a change: with lifespan instead of HP, it can deny granularity/absorb more often but leaves the opponent with 1 extra defense after it expires, compared to the current version which effectively trades with 1 defense and gets two chances to deny absorb. p0lari notes that reducing lifespan to 4 makes it harder to counter Doomed Wall/Mech or Chieftain effectively.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion during December 2020 event about redesigning Cryo Ray with lifespan instead of current HP-based design.

insight: Units with more than 3 stamina are displayable in the game UI: historically, Sentinel had 4 stamina and was shown with the full 3-bar battery graphic plus the number '4' displayed alongside it, without causing confusion.
author: elyot
context: Discussion about whether 4+ stamina units are feasible given the 3-bar stamina icon limitation in the UI.

insight: Automaid (1B version) beating Centurion was demonstrated in a replay, achieved with pure Automaid plus Gauss Cannons, suggesting the 1B cost version can defeat defensive econ strategies that would normally be stable.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 sharing a replay demonstrating the broken 1B Automaid defeating a Centurion-based economy.

insight: The presence of Automaid (1B version) and Eviscerator together effectively crowds out Big Red strategies: both units provide Big Red-level efficiency without Big Red's punishability (opponent getting 5 attack vs Big Red ends the game; vs Eviscerator/Automaid it does not), making Big Red redundant in sets where these units appear.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 noting nobody plays Big Red in event games due to Automaid and Eviscerator dominating the aggressive role.

insight: Lancetooth at 8BX cost is considered solid and fair in event games by masn6811, fitting best as a single-blue attacker. silentslayers notes that a very strong single-blue unit reduces set-reading skill by making too many sets tooth-oriented regardless of other units available.
author: consensus
context: masn6811 and silentslayers evaluating the 8BX Lancetooth variant during December 2020 event testing.

insight: elyot observes that all the degenerate Automaid interactions observed during the broken 1B event test involved either Lancetooth or Eviscerator as combo partners, suggesting Automaid alone may be closer to balanced and the problematic cases arise from specific unit synergies.
author: elyot
context: elyot summarizing observations from the broken 1B Automaid event to assess whether Automaid itself or its synergies are the core issue.

insight: In Eviscerator mirror games with sufficient attack available, spamming Eviscerators becomes viable and the self-counter dynamic from early purchases no longer applies — the early-game constraint is specifically about reaching 5 attack before the opponent's Eviscerator matures.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers clarifying the conditions under which Eviscerator's self-counter property applies versus dissolves.

insight: Cryo Ray's bot (Master Bot) behavior of wasting freeze clicks on suboptimal targets was noted as a known problem; a lifespan-based Cryo Ray redesign was partially motivated by the observation that it would at least prevent the bot from misusing it to freeze things that aren't worth freezing.
author: velizar_
context: Community discussion about redesigning Cryo Ray, with velizar_ noting a silver lining that lifespan would mitigate the bot's poor freeze targeting.
